title: cinchona scrobiculata var. delondriana
intro: Cinchona scrobiculata var. delondriana is a variant of the Cinchona plant, a species of flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America. It is a source of quinine, a natural antimalarial compound used to treat malaria and other parasitic diseases. The medicinal properties of Cinchona scrobiculata var. delondriana include its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and febrifugal properties, which have been used to alleviate fever, headaches, and other symptoms associated with malaria. The health benefits of the plant include reduced risk of malaria and other parasitic diseases, as well as relief from fever and inflammation, and it can be prepared in various herbal forms such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
